name: Golden Hour Rule
description: The Golden Hour Rule is a tactic used in crisis communications to respond to a crisis within the first hour of it becoming public. This can help to mitigate the damage and reduce the risk of the crisis escalating.
when_to_apply: In the first hour of a crisis becoming public
example: A company experiences a data breach and responds within the first hour by issuing a statement and apologizing for the incident.
anti_pattern: Waiting too long to respond to a crisis

name: Social Media Campaign
description: A social media campaign is a tactic used to raise awareness and build support for a cause or issue. This can be used in crisis communications to respond to a crisis and build support for the affected parties.
when_to_apply: In response to a crisis or issue
example: A social media campaign is launched to raise awareness about the situation in Ukraine and build support for the Ukrainian people.
anti_pattern: Ignoring social media and not using it to respond to a crisis
